2026-03-20 日経225先物 20日夜間取引終値 53000 TOPIX先物 3572
2026-03-19 TDnet サツドラ 高千穂交易 富士製薬 業績予想 配当
2026-03-20 Reuters ホルムズ海峡 日本 共同声明 BOJ 祝日休場

snapshot: TSE cash equities are closed today for Vernal Equinox Day. The next cash session is Monday, March 23.
Nikkei 225 futures finished the night session at 53,000 and TOPIX futures at 3,572, still below the last cash close on a basis-comparison view.
U.S. equities fell again on March 19. The S&P 500 closed at 6,606.49 (-0.27%), the Nasdaq at 22,090.69 (-0.28%), and the Dow at 46,021.43 (-0.44%).
After the BOJ decision, USD/JPY moved down to 157.61 in New York, reversing part of the prior 159-yen weakness.
In Asian trading on March 20, Brent was at $107.41 and WTI at $94.90. The panic bid eased, but the Hormuz supply risk remains unresolved.

drivers.driver: Cash equities are closed, so there is no same-day watchlist
drivers.why_today: Because Japan cash equities are shut for the holiday, there is no live tape, sector leadership or turnover signal to justify a midday long ranking. Today's watchlist is therefore intentionally empty.

drivers.driver: Oil, FX and central-bank repricing now matter more than stock ranking
drivers.why_today: Crude has eased from the extremes but remains elevated, while USD/JPY has moved into the 157s. Post-BOJ, post-FOMC assumptions have shifted again and will frame the next cash session.

drivers.driver: March 19 after-close TDnet disclosures now roll into Monday
drivers.why_today: Names such as Satsudora, Takachiho Koheki and Fuji Pharma filed after the March 19 close, but the holiday means cash equities have not processed those filings yet.

headline_inventory.headline: Japan's domestic cash market is closed for Vernal Equinox Day
Nikkei 225 futures ended the night session at 53,000, while TOPIX futures closed at 3,572
Brent slipped to $107.41 and WTI to $94.90, though the weekly oil backdrop is still elevated
U.S. stocks fell again on March 19, with the S&P 500 down 0.27% as rate-cut hopes were pushed further out
The yen strengthened to 157.61 per dollar as the euro and yen both rose against the U.S. dollar
The BOJ held rates at 0.75% and warned that higher oil prices could intensify inflation pressure
Japan and five European nations issued a joint Hormuz statement backing safe passage and energy-market stabilisation efforts
Satsudora Holdings disclosed a revision to its full-year earnings outlook
Takachiho Koheki disclosed a full-year outlook revision, a year-end dividend revision and a special-loss booking
Fuji Pharma disclosed a revision to its interim dividend forecast for FY ending September 2026

deep_dive.title: Holiday midday note: no cash-equity watchlist today, only a carry-over map into Monday

lines: March 20 is a Japan cash-market holiday, so there is no live order book, turnover or sector-relative tape to support a responsible midday long ranking.
The right approach is to leave the watchlist empty and update only the external backdrop and the unprocessed after-close filings that can matter on Monday.
Kabutan's daily schedule flags the domestic market as closed, while the night-session futures close still provides a reference point for next-session risk appetite.
At the same time, U.S. equities, BOJ communication, FX repricing and Hormuz headlines all moved, so the setup for March 23 is not static.
Into Monday's open, the first question is whether Brent, WTI, USD/JPY and Nikkei futures hold close to the March 20 morning levels or reprice again over the weekend.
The second is whether March 19 after-close TDnet filings, including Satsudora, Takachiho Koheki and Fuji Pharma, gain or lose relevance before cash trading resumes.
Weekend Middle East headlines could materially alter the oil, shipping and FX assumptions before Monday's cash open.
Holiday futures and FX moves should not be mapped mechanically onto individual cash equities without a fresh tape.
A holiday edition has no same-day cash price discovery, so ranking precision is intentionally lower than on a live trading day.
Because Japan now rolls into a three-day gap before the next cash session, some March 19 disclosures can lose freshness before they are actually traded.

no_trade_conditions: When the domestic cash market is closed and there is no same-day price discovery to validate stock selection
When holiday moves in futures, FX or crude are being copied mechanically into individual cash-equity ideas
When the Hormuz, oil or USD/JPY premise changes materially before Monday's cash session
When March 19 after-close TDnet filings are altered in substance by follow-up disclosure or company clarification before trading resumes
content.disclaimer: This is a holiday market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
